macro_segments.title: Magbiya Metzia L'Chaveiro: Can One Acquire a Lost Object on Behalf of Another Person?

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the Principle: Migo D'Zachi L'Nafshei (Since I Could Keep It for Myself)
The Alternative Position: Tofes L'Baal Chov B'Makom Shechav L'Acherim (Seizing for One While Others Are Owed)
Reading the Mishnah: Does the Picker Claim Original Intent for Himself?
The Significance of 'Originally' (Techila) in the Seifa: Establishing the Debate Framework
Two Positions on Magbiya Metzia: Does the Act Work or Only If Claims of Original Intent?
Rav Nachman and Rav Chisda: Magbiya Metzia L'Chaveiro Does Not Work

macro_segments.title: The Case of Hired Labor (Po'el): Distinction Between Magbiya Metzia and Shlichut When Working Under Contract

micro_segments.title: Rava's Challenge: A Day Laborer Finding a Diamond While Hired
The Distinction: Specific Task (Nechish) vs. General Employment (Asei Melacha Hayom)
Po'el as Yad Ba'al Habayis: When Labor Is Part of Overall Employment
The Right to Break Contract: Poel Yachol Lachzor Bo Even Mid-Day
Midrash from Leviticus: Ki Li Bnei Yisrael Avadim—Preventing Servitude
Rabbi Chiya Bar Abba: Magbiya Metzia L'Chaveiro Does Work—Alternative Reading
When Did He Pick It Up for You? Intent to Deliver vs. Intent to Take Possession
Tosafot's Nuance: Quasi-Servitude Acceptable Only With Right to Exit

macro_segments.title: Ra'ah Metzia Venafal Aleha: Physical Acts That Do Not Constitute Kinyan

micro_segments.title: Falling on an Object Does Not Create Kinyan; Actual Possession Is Required
Arba Amot Shel Adam Konot Lo B'Chol Makom: Four Cubits Create Possession Everywhere
Taknat Rabanan: Preventing Physical Fights Over Lost Objects by Proximity Rule

macro_segments.title: Challenges to Arba Amot: Pe'ah and Nezikin Mishnayot

micro_segments.title: Pe'ah Challenge: Throwing Pe'ah on Remaining Grain Does Not Work
The Girsa Issue: Did He Say He Wanted His Arba Amot to Acquire?
Gali Da'atei: Intent Shown Through the Act Itself Overrides Other Kinyanim
The Distinction: Falling Shows Intent for Nefilah, Not Arba Amot
Moral Dimension (Tosafot): Not Even Called Rasha When Taking from Failed Attempt

macro_segments.title: Rav Papa and Rav Sheshes: Contextual Limitations on Arba Amot Kinyan

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa: Arba Amot Only Works in a Regular Field (Besada De'Alma), Not in Owner's Field
The Logic: Your Personal Space Cannot Override Actual Owner's Possession
Rav Sheshes: Arba Amot Works in Simta (Side Street) But Not in Reshus Harabbim (Public Domain)
Dachki Rabbim: Crowded Conditions Negate Personal Space Concept

macro_segments.title: Ketana and Chatzer: Can a Minor or Personal Space Acquire Through Rabbinic Kinyan?

micro_segments.title: The Question: Can a Minor Girl Be Divorced Through Arba Amot or Chatzer?
Reish Lakish: Ketana Has Neither Chatzer Nor Arba Amot
Rabbi Yochanan: Ketana Has Both Chatzer and Arba Amot
The Debate: Is Chatzer an Extension of Person (Yad) or Agency (Shlichut)?
First Position: Chatzer Mishum Yadah Atrabai—Yard as Hand Extension
Second Position: Chatzer Mishum Shlichut—Yard Acting on Behalf

macro_segments.title: Ketana and Chatzer: Can a Minor or Personal Space Acquire Through Rabbinic Kinyan? (continued)

micro_segments.title: Implications: If Chatzer Is Shlichut, Can It Perform Aveirah (Crime)?

macro_segments.title: Ein Shaliach L'Dvar Aveirah: When Agency Cannot Represent Criminal Acts

micro_segments.title: The Proof from Gneivah: Can Chatzer Steal on My Behalf?
Ravina's Answer: Shaliach Must Be Bar Chiyuva (Obligated by Law)
The Logic: Those Bound by Prohibition Must Make Their Own Moral Choice
Chatzer Not Bar Chiyuva: Inanimate Object Cannot Choose; Acts on Your Behalf
Objection: Woman and Slave Are Also Bar Chiyuva Despite Liability Limits
Rava's Better Answer: Shaliach Only When Agent Can Choose to Refuse
Ba'al Korchah: Chatzer Acts Involuntarily; Therefore Mechayev Shalcho (Sender Liable)
